sections.title: Phase 1 — A.1: L6 Core Process Steps
sections.content:
**CFJ (industry-level):** Deliver continuous, secure, and compliant compute, storage, and network capacity to tenants and end users at contracted uptime and performance levels.

| ID | Name | Job Family | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6a | Power Supply & Conditioning | Transform and condition utility power to IT-grade uninterruptible supply at N+1 or 2N redundancy | Entry point of every DC value chain; no IT can operate without it |
| L6b | Cooling & Thermal Management | Maintain IT equipment within operating temperature and humidity limits across all load densities | Rising importance with AI/GPU workloads driving kW/rack from 7 kW to 100+ kW |
| L6c | Physical Infrastructure | Provide secure, structured rack space, cabling, and floor systems for tenant equipment | Colocation-specific; defines deployable capacity |
| L6d | Physical Security & Access Control | Control, monitor, and log physical access to all facility zones (perimeter → cage → rack) | **Primary step where Emergency Exit Door Monitoring sits** |
| L6e | Network Connectivity & Cross-Connect | Terminate diverse carrier connections; enable tenant-to-tenant and tenant-to-carrier cross-connects | Carrier-neutral DCs differentiate here |
| L6f | IT Infrastructure Operations | Deploy, operate, and monitor compute, storage, and network equipment for managed workloads | Hyperscale and managed hosting only; skipped in pure-colo output type |

Core steps follow the physical delivery sequence: L6a (power in) → L6b (heat out) → L6c (space prepared) → L6d (access controlled) → L6e (network connected) → L6f (workload running).

---

sections.title: Phase 1 — A.2: L6 Horizontal Functions
sections.content:
| ID | Name (H) | Runs Across | Distinction from Core |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6H1 | DCIM & Monitoring (H) | All L6 core steps | Does not produce capacity — enables real-time visibility into PUE, power, temperature, and uptime across all core steps |
| L6H2 | Facilities Maintenance & Engineering (H) | All L6 core steps | Preventive and corrective maintenance across power, cooling, physical infra; required for all core steps to remain operational |
| L6H3 | Compliance & Auditing (H) | All L6 core steps | SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI-DSS, GDPR — does not produce capacity but without it no core step can operate legally for regulated tenants |
| L6H4 | Fire Suppression & Life Safety (H) | All L6 core steps | Fire detection and suppression protecting equipment and personnel across all zones; cannot be skipped or the facility cannot be occupied |

> **Warning test applied:** Removing L6d (Physical Security) means the facility cannot be operated in compliance with SOC 2 CC6.4, ISO 27001 A.7.2, or CISA/FISMA physical security requirements → output (certified capacity) cannot be delivered. Core. Removing L6H3 (Compliance) means the same output cannot be sold to regulated tenants → horizontal. Test passes.

---

> **Column read:** Edge / Micro DC skips L6f (no managed compute — edge nodes are pre-configured appliances, not operated by DC staff) and typically lacks formal compliance auditing frameworks.

sections.title: Phase 2 — 04a: L4 Fill (≥ 3 L4 per L5)
sections.content:
Critical branches fully decomposed. Emergency Exit Door Monitoring branch prioritized.

### L6d — Emergency Exit Door Monitoring ⟵ Türwächter fits here as L4[1]

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| **Door Guardian Alarm Unit** | Mechanical guard housing mounted over door handle; triggers 95 dB alarm on 45° rotation; battery-powered; mechanical alarm operates at 0 V. **← Türwächter (GfS Exit Control 179/1125)** |
| Wireless Event Transmitter | 868 MHz / BLE / LoRaWAN radio module embedded in guard; broadcasts guard-state and alarm events to receiver upon trigger or state change |
| IP Gateway / Protocol Bridge | Converts RF door events to IP (REST/SNMP/BACnet-IP); required for integration with DC security platforms and SOC dashboards; addresses C6 EasyWave lock-in constraint |
| Key Override Cylinder | Profile half-cylinder or high-security keyed cylinder for authorized personnel bypass without triggering alarm; critical for data center maintenance operations |

### L6d — Biometric Access Control

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Biometric Reader | Fingerprint/iris/face capture device at door; verifies enrolled identity in <2 s |
| Door Controller / Edge Module | Edge device governing lock release, audit log generation, and alarm output; stores local access policies for offline operation |
| Physical Access Control Server (PACS) | Central identity directory, access policy engine, and tamper-evident audit log store; SOC 2 CC6.2 / ISO 27001 A.7.3 requirement |
| Locking Mechanism | EM lock, electric strike, or electric bolt; fail-safe release on power loss (compliant with EN 13637 / NFPA 101) |

### L6d — CCTV & Video Analytics

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| IP Fixed / PTZ Camera | 4K surveillance camera at perimeter, lobby, cage, row, and exit points; tamper-detection output |
| Network Video Recorder (NVR) | Video storage with ≥90-day retention; indexed for compliance audit access |
| Video Analytics Engine | Tailgating, loitering, and forced-door detection algorithms; triggers SOC alert in real time |
| Video Management Software (VMS) | Operator dashboard for live view, playback, and alert management; integrates with PACS for correlated events |

### L6a — UPS System

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Battery Module | VRLA (62% installed base) or Li-ion cells; primary energy storage; target runtime 5–15 min to bridge to generator |
| Inverter / Rectifier Assembly | AC/DC and DC/AC conversion; maintains output voltage during utility disturbance |
| Static Bypass Switch | Maintenance bypass isolating UPS from critical load path without outage; required for scheduled maintenance |
| UPS Controller / BMS | Monitors battery state-of-health (SOH) and state-of-charge (SOC); communicates status to DCIM; triggers early-warning alarms |

### L6b — Chiller Plant

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Compressor | Centrifugal or screw compressor; primary thermal lift; largest energy consumer in cooling chain |
| Heat Exchanger (Evaporator/Condenser) | Chilled water and condenser water interface; transfers heat from IT load to condenser loop |
| Variable Speed Drive (VSD) | Motor speed control for compressor and pump; enables part-load energy optimization (PUE benefit) |
| Chiller Controller | PLC/DDC unit; optimizes compressor loading, free-cooling economizer switchover, and alarm management |

### L6b — Liquid Cooling System

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Coolant Distribution Unit (CDU) | Pumps and heat exchangers distributing dielectric fluid or water directly to server heat sinks; primary active component |
| Quick-Disconnect Server Manifold | Blind-mate connectors at rack level; enables hot-swap of servers without coolant spill |
| Leak Detection Sensor | Point-of-use or rope-style leak sensor below raised floor and at manifold joints; triggers shutdown before coolant contacts electronics |

### L6e — Cross-Connect Infrastructure

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Fiber Patch Panel | Physical termination point for fiber cross-connects; density ≥96 ports per 1U |
| Optical Distribution Frame (ODF) | Organized termination for customer circuits; labeled for audit trail per colocation SLA |
| Cross-Connect Order Management | Software layer tracking provisioned circuits, customer authorizations, and change requests; SOC 2 CC6.6 access change log |

### L6H4 — Clean Agent Suppression

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Agent Storage Cylinder | Pressurized FM-200/Novec/Inergen cylinder; sized per protected zone volume per NFPA 2001 |
| Detection Control Panel | Dual-detector (smoke + heat) pre-discharge alarm; two-sensor cross-zone prevents false discharge |
| Discharge Nozzle | Fixed nozzle array distributing agent uniformly across protected volume within 10 s of activation |

sections.title: Türwächter VN Anchor
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **VN Anchor Level** | L4 |
| **L4 Node** | Door Guardian Alarm Unit |
| **Parent L5** | Emergency Exit Door Monitoring |
| **Parent L6** | Physical Security & Access Control (L6d) |
| **CFJ Contribution** | Fulfills the fire-code compliance and SOC 2 CC6.4 sub-job within L6d: ensuring emergency exit doors generate an audit-ready access-event log while remaining instantaneously usable for evacuation under all power conditions |
| **Output types served** | Universal — all 4 data center output types require L6d Physical Security |
| **Architecture distance** | 3 (MEDIUM) — physical fit is good (standard server room doors); primary barrier is C6 (EasyWave proprietary protocol; DCs expect REST/SNMP/BACnet-IP) and C3 (RF signal attenuation through server racks and shielded rooms) |

### Constraint Overlay (data center specific)

| Constraint | Impact in this market | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| C6 EasyWave Protocol Lock-In | **Primary barrier** — Data centers mandate IP-native, API-first integration with PACS/SIEM platforms. EasyWave to IP bridging required (IP Gateway L4 node above) | GfS wireless BUS gateway + IP converter; REST API adapter layer |
| C3 Wireless Range | Metal server racks and shielded rooms (Faraday cage effect) attenuate 868 MHz. Typical DC aisles are <30 m but RF propagation is degraded | Repeaters + antenna placement at aisle ends; site RF survey required |
| C5 Surface Mount | Acceptable — server room doors are standard commercial doors; aesthetic constraint does not apply | No action needed |
| C8 GDPR | Door-event timestamps logged to SIEM create compliance data records; EU DC operators need DPA in place | DPA with SIEM provider; data minimization (no personnel identity in event log) |
| C2 Mechanical Alarm Independence | **Pre-satisfied** — Battery-operated mechanical alarm continues during DC power events when emergency exits are most critical; this is a selling point, not a risk | Highlight as key differentiator vs. mains-powered exit controllers |

### Competitive Position at L5 (Emergency Exit Door Monitoring)

| Alternative | Share est. | Türwächter advantage | Türwächter disadvantage |
|---|---|---|---|
| Wired electronic exit alarm, alarm-panel-integrated | ~50% | No wiring required; retrofit-ready; battery fail-safe | Protocol lock-in vs. IP-native wired solutions |
| Wireless IoT door alarm guard (Türwächter category) | ~30% | Dual-standard (EN 179 + EN 1125); mechanical fail-safe; 95 dB | EasyWave vs. BACnet/IP gap; surface mount only |
| Mechanical-only door prop alarm | ~20% | Full compliance logging + remote monitoring vs. no monitoring | Higher cost; requires IP gateway for DC compliance |

{
  "naics_code": "518210",
  "naics_title": "Computing Infrastructure Providers, Data Processing, Web Hosting, and Related Services",
  "cfj_statement": "Deliver continuous, secure, and compliant compute, storage, and network capacity to tenants and end users at contracted uptime and performance levels",
  "l7_solution": "Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem",
  "units": [
    { "name": "Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem", "level": "L7", "description": "Moore 1993 ecosystem — the complete community of operators, suppliers, tenants, regulators, and carriers coordinated to deliver digital infrastructure services", "bom_standard": "Moore 1993", "parent_name": "ROOT" },

    { "name": "Power Supply & Conditioning", "level": "L6", "description": "Transform and condition utility power to IT-grade uninterruptible supply at N+1 or 2N redundancy", "bom_standard": "Maier 1998", "parent_name": "Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem" },
    { "name": "Cooling & Thermal Management", "level": "L6", "description": "Maintain IT equipment within operating temperature and humidity limits across all load densities", "bom_standard": "Maier 1998", "parent_name": "Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem" },
    { "name": "Physical Infrastructure", "level": "L6", "description": "Provide secure, structured rack space, cabling, and floor systems for tenant equipment", "bom_standard": "Maier 1998", "parent_name": "Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem" },
    { "name": "Physical Security & Access Control", "level": "L6", "description": "Control, monitor, and log physical access to all facility zones — perimeter, lobby, colo floor, cage, row, rack", "bom_standard": "Maier 1998", "parent_name": "Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem" },
    { "name": "Network Connectivity & Cross-Connect", "level": "L6", "description": "Terminate diverse carrier connections and enable tenant-to-tenant and tenant-to-carrier cross-connects", "bom_standard": "Maier 1998", "parent_name": "Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem" },
    { "name": "IT Infrastructure Operations", "level": "L6", "description": "Deploy, operate, and monitor compute, storage, and network equipment for managed workloads (hyperscale and managed hosting only)", "bom_standard": "Maier 1998", "parent_name": "Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem" },
    { "name": "DCIM & Monitoring", "level": "L6", "description": "Horizontal — real-time visibility into PUE, power, temperature, and uptime across all core steps", "bom_standard": "Maier 1998", "parent_name": "Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem" },
    { "name": "Facilities Maintenance & Engineering", "level": "L6", "description": "Horizontal — preventive and corrective maintenance across all power, cooling, and physical infrastructure", "bom_standard": "Maier 1998", "parent_name": "Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem" },
    { "name": "Compliance & Auditing", "level": "L6", "description": "Horizontal — SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI-DSS, GDPR compliance across all steps", "bom_standard": "Maier 1998", "parent_name": "Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem" },
    { "name": "Fire Suppression & Life Safety", "level": "L6", "description": "Horizontal — fire detection and suppression protecting equipment and personnel across all zones", "bom_standard": "Maier 1998", "parent_name": "Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem" },

    { "name": "Medium Voltage Switchgear", "level": "L5", "description": "Switches and protection gear for incoming medium-voltage utility feed; first point of power control", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Power Supply & Conditioning" },
    { "name": "Transformer", "level": "L5", "description": "Steps utility voltage down to LV distribution level; dry-type or oil-immersed", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Power Supply & Conditioning" },
    { "name": "UPS System", "level": "L5", "description": "Uninterruptible power supply maintaining clean power during utility disturbances; VRLA or Li-ion battery", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Power Supply & Conditioning" },
    { "name": "Emergency Diesel Generator", "level": "L5", "description": "On-site generation providing runtime power after UPS bridge time; diesel or gas-powered", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Power Supply & Conditioning" },
    { "name": "Automatic Transfer Switch", "level": "L5", "description": "Switches critical load between utility and generator within milliseconds on utility failure", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Power Supply & Conditioning" },
    { "name": "Power Distribution Unit", "level": "L5", "description": "Rack- or floor-level distribution of conditioned power to servers; intelligent iPDU enables per-outlet monitoring", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Power Supply & Conditioning" },

    { "name": "Chiller Plant", "level": "L5", "description": "Produces chilled water for CRAH cooling coils; centrifugal or magnetic bearing compressor", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Cooling & Thermal Management" },
    { "name": "Cooling Tower / Dry Cooler", "level": "L5", "description": "Rejects condenser heat to atmosphere; evaporative or dry-cooler type", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Cooling & Thermal Management" },
    { "name": "Room Air Handler", "level": "L5", "description": "CRAH/CRAC units distributing conditioned air within server halls; chilled water or DX coil", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Cooling & Thermal Management" },
    { "name": "Liquid Cooling System", "level": "L5", "description": "Direct-to-chip or immersion cooling for high-density GPU/CPU racks; fastest-growing segment", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Cooling & Thermal Management" },
    { "name": "Hot/Cold Aisle Containment", "level": "L5", "description": "Physical separation of hot and cold air streams to improve cooling efficiency and PUE", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Cooling & Thermal Management" },

    { "name": "Server Rack & Cabinet", "level": "L5", "description": "Standardized 42U–48U open or enclosed frames for mounting tenant equipment", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Physical Infrastructure" },
    { "name": "Structured Cabling", "level": "L5", "description": "Fiber and copper cabling infrastructure connecting racks to patch panels and network equipment", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Physical Infrastructure" },
    { "name": "Floor Infrastructure", "level": "L5", "description": "Raised floor or slab-plus-overhead-tray system managing airflow and cable routing below/above racks", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Physical Infrastructure" },
    { "name": "Cage & Compartment Barrier", "level": "L5", "description": "Wire mesh or solid panel partitions separating tenant cages within shared colo floor", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Physical Infrastructure" },

    { "name": "Perimeter Security", "level": "L5", "description": "Anti-ram bollards, fencing, and perimeter intrusion detection forming the outermost physical security layer", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Physical Security & Access Control" },
    { "name": "Biometric Access Control", "level": "L5", "description": "Multi-factor identity verification at all controlled entry points; fingerprint, iris, or facial recognition", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Physical Security & Access Control" },
    { "name": "CCTV & Video Analytics", "level": "L5", "description": "Continuous surveillance with AI analytics for tailgating, loitering, and forced-door detection", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Physical Security & Access Control" },
    { "name": "Security Mantrap / Interlock", "level": "L5", "description": "Airlock-style vestibule preventing tailgating between lobby and colo floor; mandatory in Tier III/IV DCs", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Physical Security & Access Control" },
    { "name": "Emergency Exit Door Monitoring", "level": "L5", "description": "Detection and logging of emergency exit door activations for SOC 2 CC6.4 and ISO 27001 A.7.2 audit trails; battery-operated monitoring required for power-event coverage", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Physical Security & Access Control" },
    { "name": "Security Operations Center", "level": "L5", "description": "Centralized console for guard staff monitoring all physical security feeds; on-site or remote managed service", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Physical Security & Access Control" },

    { "name": "Carrier Fiber Termination", "level": "L5", "description": "Dark fiber or lit wavelength entry points from diverse carrier paths; minimum two geographically separate entries", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Network Connectivity & Cross-Connect" },
    { "name": "Cross-Connect Infrastructure", "level": "L5", "description": "Fiber patch panels enabling tenant-to-tenant and tenant-to-carrier physical cross-connects", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Network Connectivity & Cross-Connect" },
    { "name": "DDoS & Traffic Scrubbing", "level": "L5", "description": "On-premise or upstream mitigation of volumetric denial-of-service traffic protecting tenant network connectivity", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Network Connectivity & Cross-Connect" },

    { "name": "Compute Servers", "level": "L5", "description": "Custom ODM or OEM servers delivering compute capacity for hyperscale or managed workloads", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "IT Infrastructure Operations" },
    { "name": "Storage Arrays", "level": "L5", "description": "All-flash NVMe or hybrid storage systems; object storage nodes for unstructured data", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "IT Infrastructure Operations" },
    { "name": "Network Switching", "level": "L5", "description": "Leaf-spine switching fabric using merchant silicon or custom ASICs; 400G/800G port speeds", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "IT Infrastructure Operations" },

    { "name": "DCIM Software Platform", "level": "L5", "description": "Data Center Infrastructure Management platform providing real-time PUE, capacity, and asset visibility", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "DCIM & Monitoring" },
    { "name": "Environmental Sensor Array", "level": "L5", "description": "Distributed temperature, humidity, and airflow sensors reporting to DCIM; BACnet/SNMP/Modbus protocols", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "DCIM & Monitoring" },
    { "name": "Power Monitoring System", "level": "L5", "description": "Branch-circuit and panel-level power meters feeding real-time kW data to DCIM and tenant billing systems", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "DCIM & Monitoring" },

    { "name": "CMMS Platform", "level": "L5", "description": "Computerized Maintenance Management System scheduling preventive maintenance and tracking work orders", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Facilities Maintenance & Engineering" },
    { "name": "Diagnostic & Test Equipment", "level": "L5", "description": "Thermal cameras, power quality analyzers, and vibration sensors for predictive maintenance routines", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Facilities Maintenance & Engineering" },

    { "name": "SIEM & Audit Log Platform", "level": "L5", "description": "Security Information and Event Management collecting and correlating physical and logical access logs; SOC 2 CC7.2 / ISO 27001 A.8.15 requirement", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Compliance & Auditing" },
    { "name": "GRC Platform", "level": "L5", "description": "Governance, Risk, and Compliance platform managing control registers, evidence collection, and audit workflows", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Compliance & Auditing" },

    { "name": "Clean Agent Suppression", "level": "L5", "description": "FM-200, Novec 1230, or Inergen total-flooding system protecting IT zones without water damage", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Fire Suppression & Life Safety" },
    { "name": "Early Warning Smoke Detection", "level": "L5", "description": "VESDA aspirating or addressable detectors providing pre-fire detection before visible smoke", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Fire Suppression & Life Safety" },
    { "name": "Sprinkler System", "level": "L5", "description": "Pre-action dry-pipe sprinklers in IT areas; wet pipe in non-IT mechanical areas; per NFPA 13 / FM Global", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "Fire Suppression & Life Safety" },

    { "name": "Door Guardian Alarm Unit", "level": "L4", "description": "Mechanical guard housing over door handle; 95 dB alarm on 45° rotation; battery-powered; mechanical alarm operates at 0 V — fail-safe for power events. GfS Exit Control 179/1125 (Türwächter)", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Emergency Exit Door Monitoring" },
    { "name": "Wireless Event Transmitter", "level": "L4", "description": "868 MHz / BLE / LoRaWAN radio module broadcasting guard-state and alarm events to receiver upon trigger", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Emergency Exit Door Monitoring" },

    { "name": "PACS Backend", "level": "L4", "description": "Central identity directory, access policy engine, and tamper-evident audit log store; SOC 2 CC6.2 / ISO 27001 A.7.3 requirement", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Biometric Access Control" },
    { "name": "Locking Mechanism", "level": "L4", "description": "EM lock, electric strike, or electric bolt; fail-safe release on power loss per EN 13637 / NFPA 101", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Biometric Access Control" },

    { "name": "IP Fixed / PTZ Camera", "level": "L4", "description": "4K surveillance camera at perimeter, lobby, cage row, and exit points; tamper-detection output", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "CCTV & Video Analytics" },
    { "name": "Network Video Recorder", "level": "L4", "description": "Video storage with ≥90-day retention; indexed for compliance audit replay", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "CCTV & Video Analytics" },
    { "name": "Video Analytics Engine", "level": "L4", "description": "Tailgating, loitering, and forced-door detection algorithms; real-time SOC alert trigger", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "CCTV & Video Analytics" },
    { "name": "Video Management Software", "level": "L4", "description": "Operator dashboard for live view, playback, and alert management; integrates with PACS for correlated events", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "CCTV & Video Analytics" },

    { "name": "Battery Module", "level": "L4", "description": "VRLA or Li-ion cells; energy storage for UPS bridge time (5–15 min); 62% installed base VRLA, Li-ion growing at 8.29% CAGR", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "UPS System" },

    { "name": "Heat Exchanger", "level": "L4", "description": "Evaporator and condenser coil transferring heat between chilled water and condenser loops", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Chiller Plant" },

    { "name": "Fiber Patch Panel", "level": "L4", "description": "Physical termination point for fiber cross-connects; ≥96 ports per 1U", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Cross-Connect Infrastructure" },

    { "name": "Cross-Connect Order Management", "level": "L4", "description": "Software tracking provisioned circuits, customer authorizations, and change requests; SOC 2 CC6.6 access change log", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Cross-Connect Infrastructure" },

    { "name": "Agent Storage Cylinder", "level": "L4", "description": "Pressurized FM-200/Novec/Inergen cylinder sized per protected zone volume per NFPA 2001", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Clean Agent Suppression" },

    { "name": "Discharge Nozzle", "level": "L4", "description": "Fixed nozzle array distributing agent uniformly within protected volume in ≤10 s of activation", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Clean Agent Suppression" }

rows.Value: Computing Infrastructure Providers, Data Processing, Web Hosting, and Related Services
NO (service NAICS — output is compute/storage/network capacity, not a physical product)

rows.Name: Power Supply & Conditioning
rows.Job Family: Transform and condition utility power to IT-grade uninterruptible supply at N+1 or 2N redundancy
rows.Notes: Entry point of every DC value chain; no IT can operate without it

rows.Name: Cooling & Thermal Management
rows.Job Family: Maintain IT equipment within operating temperature and humidity limits across all load densities
rows.Notes: Rising importance with AI/GPU workloads driving kW/rack from 7 kW to 100+ kW

rows.Name: Physical Infrastructure
rows.Job Family: Provide secure, structured rack space, cabling, and floor systems for tenant equipment

rows.Name: Physical Security & Access Control
rows.Job Family: Control, monitor, and log physical access to all facility zones (perimeter → cage → rack)
rows.Notes: **Primary step where Emergency Exit Door Monitoring sits**

rows.Name: Network Connectivity & Cross-Connect
rows.Job Family: Terminate diverse carrier connections; enable tenant-to-tenant and tenant-to-carrier cross-connects

rows.Name: IT Infrastructure Operations
rows.Job Family: Deploy, operate, and monitor compute, storage, and network equipment for managed workloads
rows.Notes: Hyperscale and managed hosting only; skipped in pure-colo output type

rows.Distinction from Core: Does not produce capacity — enables real-time visibility into PUE, power, temperature, and uptime across all core steps
Preventive and corrective maintenance across power, cooling, physical infra; required for all core steps to remain operational
SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI-DSS, GDPR — does not produce capacity but without it no core step can operate legally for regulated tenants
Fire detection and suppression protecting equipment and personnel across all zones; cannot be skipped or the facility cannot be occupied

rows.Alternatives: 1. AIS air-insulated (~60%) · 2. GIS gas-insulated (~30%) · 3. Ring main unit (~10%)
1. Dry-type step-down (~60%) · 2. Oil-immersed (~30%) · 3. Cast resin (~10%)
1. Static VRLA (~62%) · 2. Static Li-ion (~25%) · 3. Rotary/diesel UPS (~8%) · 4. Flywheel UPS (~5%)
rows.Source: MarketsandMarkets 2025; Precedence Research [[SRC: VRLA 62% / Li-ion ~25% as of 2025; Li-ion CAGR 8.29%]]

rows.Alternatives: 1. Diesel reciprocating (~85%) · 2. Natural gas (~10%) · 3. HVO/biodiesel (~5% growing)
1. Static ATS (~55%) · 2. Mechanical ATS (~35%) · 3. Bypass-isolation ATS (~10%)
1. Intelligent iPDU (~55%) · 2. Basic metered PDU (~30%) · 3. Overhead busway distribution (~15%)

rows.Description: Mechanical guard housing mounted over door handle; triggers 95 dB alarm on 45° rotation; battery-powered; mechanical alarm operates at 0 V. **← Türwächter (GfS Exit Control 179/1125)**

rows.Missing / Gap: L6g Asset Decommission / Data Destruction absent — low relevance to Türwächter; acceptable gap
Site Utilities (water, backup connectivity, fuel logistics) absent as horizontal — relevant for large DCs; acceptable gap for Türwächter scope
SOC 2 CC6.4 physical security evidence log → confirmed in SIEM L4
BESS (Battery Energy Storage System) growing segment absent; add as optional L5 under L6a
Immersion cooling specifics minimal; acceptable for VN scope
HIPAA, PCI-DSS physical addenda not explicitly modeled; in scope of GRC platform

rows.Reason: Industry term for RF-to-IP bridging; used in building automation and DC security integration contexts
"Physical Access Control Server" is correct domain term; parenthetical in doc only
CDU is the domain-standard abbreviation; Schneider, Vertiv, and Emerson all use it
IEC/TIA-942 standard term; universally recognized in DC cabling
VESDA (Very Early Smoke Detection Apparatus) is the industry-standard generic term for aspirating detectors
Functional category name, not brand; consistent with other VN documents in this project

rows.Notes: All units use L7, L6, L5, or L4 only
Every non-L7 unit references a valid parent
L7→L6→L5→L4 only; no level skipping; service NAICS does not require L6a sub-SoS groupings
All L5 nodes have ≥ 3 L4 children (critical branches) or ≥ 2 (remaining table)
Above 20–40 soft target; justified by data center complexity (10 L6 nodes × 3–6 L5 each + L4 fill); within extended acceptable range

rows.Value: Fulfills the fire-code compliance and SOC 2 CC6.4 sub-job within L6d: ensuring emergency exit doors generate an audit-ready access-event log while remaining instantaneously usable for evacuation under all power conditions
Universal — all 4 data center output types require L6d Physical Security

3 (MEDIUM) — physical fit is good (standard server room doors); primary barrier is C6 (EasyWave proprietary protocol; DCs expect REST/SNMP/BACnet-IP) and C3 (RF signal attenuation through server racks and shielded rooms)

rows.Impact in this market: **Primary barrier** — Data centers mandate IP-native, API-first integration with PACS/SIEM platforms. EasyWave to IP bridging required (IP Gateway L4 node above)
rows.Mitigation: GfS wireless BUS gateway + IP converter; REST API adapter layer

rows.Impact in this market: Metal server racks and shielded rooms (Faraday cage effect) attenuate 868 MHz. Typical DC aisles are <30 m but RF propagation is degraded
rows.Mitigation: Repeaters + antenna placement at aisle ends; site RF survey required

rows.Impact in this market: Acceptable — server room doors are standard commercial doors; aesthetic constraint does not apply
Door-event timestamps logged to SIEM create compliance data records; EU DC operators need DPA in place
rows.Mitigation: DPA with SIEM provider; data minimization (no personnel identity in event log)

rows.Impact in this market: **Pre-satisfied** — Battery-operated mechanical alarm continues during DC power events when emergency exits are most critical; this is a selling point, not a risk
rows.Mitigation: Highlight as key differentiator vs. mains-powered exit controllers

rows.Türwächter advantage: No wiring required; retrofit-ready; battery fail-safe
rows.Türwächter disadvantage: Protocol lock-in vs. IP-native wired solutions

rows.Alternative: Wireless IoT door alarm guard (Türwächter category)
rows.Türwächter disadvantage: EasyWave vs. BACnet/IP gap; surface mount only

rows.Türwächter advantage: Full compliance logging + remote monitoring vs. no monitoring
rows.Türwächter disadvantage: Higher cost; requires IP gateway for DC compliance

rows.Notes: "Deliver continuous, secure, and compliant compute, storage, and network capacity…" — no product or technology reference
"Data Center Infrastructure Ecosystem" (L7); L6 names describe operational functions, not technologies
Door Guardian Alarm Unit, Coolant Distribution Unit, PACS Backend, VESDA Control Panel, Emergency Diesel Generator — all domain-standard
~90 units; data center is an exceptionally multi-system industry; 10 L6 nodes justified by ISO/IEC 22237 (data center reference architecture); all units genuine
